info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Payslips/Attachments/Attachment Previews/Attachments/Large Object (LOB) Attributes - FileWebImage
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

parameters.name: payslipsUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Payslips resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Payslips. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Payslips collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Payslips to get the hash key.

parameters.name: documentsUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Attachments resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Attachments. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Attachments collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Attachments to get the hash key.

parameters.name: AttachmentsPreviewUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Attachment Previews resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Attachment Previews. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Attachment Previews collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Attachment Previews to get the hash key.

parameters.name: AttachmentUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Attachments resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Attachments. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Attachments collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Attachments to get the hash key.
